channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Transgender people are less than 1% of the US population. Yet they’re a political lightning rod
item.description: For transgender people and their allies — along with several judges who have ruled against Trump in response to legal challenges — it’s a matter of civil rights for a small group. But many Americans believe those rights had grown too expansive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Steelers coach Mike Tomlin is talking to Aaron Rodgers, but he's comfortable starting Mason Rudolph
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Mike Tomlin is staying in touch with Aaron Rodgers and is comfortable with Mason Rudolph as the starting quarterback for the Pittsburgh Steelers. “That’s why we brought him back,” Tomlin said Monday at the league's annual meetings. “I’m comfortable with that.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pirates' shaky start to season something not seen since 1920s
item.description: The Pittsburgh Pirates have gotten off to a shaky start in 2025, losing three of their first four games, all by walk-offs from the Miami Marlins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Sox's Rafael Devers brutal start to MLB season results in unfortunate history
item.description: The Red Sox's season has gotten off to a disappointing start, but the rough beginning has been exacerbated by designated hitter Rafael Devers' alarming number of strikeouts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kerfoot has a goal and 2 assists in Utah's 5-2 victory over the Blackhawks
item.description: Alexander Kerfoot had a goal and two assists and Karel Vejmelka stopped 27 shots to help the Utah Hockey Club beat the Chicago Blackhawks 5-2 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Auburn completes sweep of No. 1 seeds into Final Four, beating Michigan State 70-64
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Johni Broome held his injured right arm through most of Auburn’s Elite Eight postgame celebration. That didn’t keep the star forward from climbing a ladder to cut down the net he then wore around his neck.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 players, 2 coaches ejected after Pistons-Timberwolves skirmish in 2nd quarter
item.description: Five players and two coaches were ejected after a fight broke out in the second quarter in the Minnesota Timberwolves’ victory over the Detroit Pistons on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Donnelly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Min Woo Lee holds off Scheffler and Woodland to win Houston Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Min Woo Lee kept his calm amid tremendous charges by Scottie Scheffler and Gary Woodland, winning the Houston Open on Sunday for his first PGA Tour title with the best lag of his life that set up a final par for a one-shot victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Denny Hamlin ends 10-year win drought at Martinsville Speedway
item.description: Denny Hamlin ended an agonizing 10-year winless streak at Martinsville Speedway, holding off teammate Christopher Bell in his home state to secure a spot in the NASCAR Cup Series playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge homers again, Chisholm Jr. goes deep twice as Yanks rout Brewers 12-3 to complete 3-game sweep
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Aaron Judge homered in his first at-bat, Jazz Chisholm Jr. homered twice and the New York Yankees went deep four more times in a 12-3 victory over the Milwaukee Brewers on Sunday, a day after becoming the third team in major league history to hit nine homers in a game.
item.dc:creator: LARRY FLEISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Mexico taps UC San Diego's Eric Olen to replace Richard Pitino
item.description: New Mexico hired UC San Diego's Eric Olen on Sunday as its next basketball coach. Olen will replace Richard Pitino, who left to become Xavier's coach on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why Dre Greenlaw left for Denver, despite the 49ers’ best efforts
item.description: 49ers general manager John Lynch and head coach Kyle Shanahan flew out to Texas to pitch their case. But the linebacker picked the Broncos.
item.dc:creator: Luca Evans, The Mercury News

item.title: Duke and Florida move on to Final Four
item.description: Florida and Duke won their regions on Saturday, with fellow top seeds Houston and Auburn set to play on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US dominated figure skating's world championships. It may face the Russians at the Olympics
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — It's hard to imagine a better world championships for the American figure skating team, which captured gold in three of the four disciplines over the weekend for the first time in its history, and will take all of that momentum into an Olympic year.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Golden Knights overcome Donato's hat trick to beat Blackhawks 5-3 for 5th straight win
item.description: Pavel Dorofeyev scored a power-play goal with 3:10 left to snap a tie, and the Vegas Golden Knights rallied for their fifth straight win, 5-3 over the Chicago Blackhawks on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gianna Kneepkens enters transfer portal
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Gianna Kneepkens, the leading scorer on the Utah women's basketball team has announced she is entering the transfer portal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Giddey's half-court buzzer beater completes miraculous comeback against Lakers, 119-117
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Josh Giddey could feel it as soon as the ball left his fingertips. In a flash, teammates were mobbing him.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Padres rally in seventh inning to win home opener against Braves
item.description: The Padres kicked off the 2025-2026 season with a comeback victory over the Atlanta Braves Thursday at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: SF's Chase Center hosting NCAA Men's Basketball games Thursday, Saturday
item.description: (BCN) -- San Francisco is hosting two games at Chase Center on Thursday in the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship tournament. At 4:39 p.m., University of Florida, which is the No. 1 seed in the West region of the tournament's bracket, plays No. 4 University of Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Opening Day 2025: Cubs have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: BYU expects shootout against Alabama in Sweet 16
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (ABC4 Sports) - The last time BYU was in the Sweet 16 was 2011 with Jimmer Fredette. For the local BYU players who grew up in Utah, they remember it well.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Late boxing legend George Foreman had deep ties to this East Bay city
item.description: (KRON) -- Boxing legend and former heavyweight champion George Foreman died last Friday at the age of 76. Foreman was born in Texas. However, after a troubled upbringing, he then moved to the West Coast after ninth grade when he enrolled in President Lyndon B. Johnson's Job Corps work program.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Bears sign DB Nick McCloud to 1-year contract
item.description: The Chicago Bears have signed defensive back Nick McCloud to a one-year contract, bringing his NFL experience from the Bengals, Giants, and 49ers to the team.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Padres Opening Day: What's new inside Petco Park
item.description: The baseball season has finally returned and San Diego Padres fans are getting amped to set foot back inside Petco Park. Here's what is new for fans inside the stadium.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
